title: /etc/containers/registries.conf documentation/links are lacking
body:
**Description**

@pixdrift pointed out that there's not a whole lot of info on /etc/containers/registries.conf that's availain in the documentation for Buildah.  A man page or other documentation should be created/referenced that explains what the contents of the file are and what the values should be.  Also a little info on how the file is used by the tools.


**Output of `buildah version`:**
Buildah v0.16

comments.body: @pixdrift, I raised this issue based on our last bit of IRC chat.  Sorry had to step away and missed the last 10 minutes or so of that.

buildah.md has 


**registries.conf** (`/etc/containers/registries.conf`)

	registries.conf is the configuration file which specifies which registries should be consulted when completing image names which do not include a registry or domain portion.

Perhaps we should also add this to bud, from, pull, push man pages along with a reference to registries.conf(5)

To follow this up, the other piece that was missing was documentation for auth.json.

From documentation here:
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/4743c2e7f132010a01de2d9cf96d10244fced423/docs/buildah-push.md

> **--authfile path**
> 
> Path of the authentication file. Default is ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json, which is set using podman login. If the authorization state is not found there, $HOME/.docker/config.json is checked, which is set using docker login.

"Path of the authentication file" is a little ambiguous, and doesn't explain what it is the authentication file _for_. It was also difficult to determine expected format without downloading podman to create it with `podman login`.

Another minor item, `atomic-registries` isn't in `Requires` for the buildah .rpm (not necessarily suggesting it needs to be) so it's not guaranteed that the end user will have an example `/etc/containers/registries.conf` file to work from after installing the buildah .rpm.

-edit-

Adding `atomic-registries` to `Requires` for buildah rpm adds 52 kB to installed size after it also brings in python-pytoml.
```
atomic-registries-1.20.1-4.el7.x86_64.rpm (34kB)
python2-pytoml-0.1.14-2.git7dea353.el7.noarch.rpm (18 kB)
```

Might be worth adding to the rpm. Thoughts @TomSweeneyRedHat? I can raise the PR to update the spec.
The registries.conf file is moving into skopeo-containers.  Which buildah does require.

The only hiccup for skopeo-containers is on ubuntu.  It's not available as a package in the two latest versions, it's a WIP.  It can be pulled from GitHub and built though, just no readily accessible rpm's there.
I think we are all set with this.  Reopen if you disagree.
I'm good, @pixdrift holler if the recent man page changes don't address this in your thinking.
